title: [DRAFT] BitNet b1.58 ternary QAT + depth recurrence submission
author: devferrazZz

data.description: K=4 unique transformer blocks applied N=6 times during training and M=12 times at evaluation to increase effective depth
Residual anchor mixing to stabilize representations across recurrence steps and prevent distribution shift at eval time
Export embeddings in FP16 with weight tying
Use of Rotary Positional Embeddings, GQA, QK-Norm, and squared ReLU activations

novel_contributions: Use of ternary quantization-aware training (QAT) with STE and learnable scale (LSQ) to achieve ~1.7 bits/param
Depth recurrence with eval-time loop scaling to effectively double model depth at evaluation without increasing parameters
Residual anchor mixing (resid_mix) to stabilize representations across recurrence steps and prevent distribution shift
Achieving ~4-5x more unique parameters in the same ~14.2MB artifact budget compared to int8 baseline
Sliding window evaluation with stride=64 for improved evaluation
FP16 tied embedding export to reduce artifact size
